title: Climbing in the Gorge
summary:
Leaving the Alabama Hills we topped up on the cheapest gas we could find and headed into Bishop. We looked around for a campsite in the Pleasant Valley area and then headed up to the Owens River Gorge for a day of climbing. I've been wanting to climb at the Owens River Gorge for a long time so I was excited to finally get to do it. We were met with an extremely steep trail from the parking lot down into the gorge, but we were rewarded with a pretty view of the Owens River and a crag all to ourselves. That night after a fun day of climbing we camped with Abby. Helen made tacos for dinner that we all shared and then we watched the TV show Beef on the projector. The following morning we headed to Bishop to check out their world famous bakery and the gear exchange. I spent a good chunk of our months dining out budget on a breakfast of a bear claw, a ham and cheese croissant and a buttermilk doughnut, which I ate in one sitting. That afternoon we returned to the Owens River Gorge for another day of awesome climbing. We climbed numerous 30 to 40 meter pitches and it was awesome to climb some long routes. For camp that night, we drove up to Pine Creek, which is another climbing area with some dispersed camping nearby. We ended up camping next to a huge group of climbers and I practiced being social and went and chatted with them by the campfire. The next morning we weren't able to climb because it was raining, but I went on a little run before we hit the road to go further north into California.

-Colin

Helen topping out a lead climb!
The view up the canyon at Pine Creek
